sections.paragraph:
Ἰεριχώ, ἡ (also Ἱερ-, Ἰερειχώ, Ἱερειχώ) indecl. (יְרִיחוֹ; LXX; Just., D. 111, 4. On the word s. W-S. §10, 1a. Joseph. varies betw. Ἱεριχώ, gen.-οῦς and Ἱεριχοῦς, gen.-οῦντος; s. Niese index. Ἰεριχοῦς, -οῦντος also Galen XI 693 K. and Steph. Byz. s.v., but in Strabo 16, 2, 41 Ἰερικοῦς. On the spelling s. B-D-F §38) Jericho, a city in Judea, not far from the ford across the Jordan just north of the Dead Sea Mt 20:29; Mk 10:46; Lk 18:35; Hb 11:30; 1 Cl 12:2. Since a much-traveled road led to Jerusalem, 150 stades (Jos., Bell. 4, 474) or about 30 km. away, a customs house was located here Lk 19:1. The road fr. Jerusalem to Jericho, which leads through desolate mountainous country (Jos., loc. cit. ἔρημον κ. πετρῶδες), was notoriously unsafe Lk 10:30 (AvVeldhuizen, TSt 25, 1907, 41–43).—ESellin and CWatzinger, Jericho 1913; Dalman, Orte3 257ff; PThomsen, Jericho: Reallex. d. Vorgesch. VI 196, 153ff; JPritchard, The 1951 Campaign at Herodian Jer.: BASOR no. 123, ’51, 8–17; JKelso, NT Jericho: BA 14, ’51, 34–43; LMowry, ibid. 15, ’52, 26–42.—BHHW II 816–19. OEANE III 220–24.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

betw. betw. = between

gen. gen. = genitive

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. km. = kilometer
  2. fr. = from

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

TSt TSt = Theologische Studien—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

BASOR BASOR = Bulletin of the American Schools of Oriental Research—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

OEANE OEANE = Oxford Encyclopedia of Archaeology in the Near East—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 469
